name: Claude / Anthropic (API Key)
costPerformanceNotes: Use API key from console.anthropic.com. Pay-per-token billing.

userFacing.plainDescription: AI research company. Their Claude models excel at analysis, writing, and code.
userFacing.authExplained: API key — a password you get from their website (console.anthropic.com).
userFacing.costExplained: Charges per use. A short conversation costs less than $0.01. Complex analysis might cost $0.05-0.20.
userFacing.capabilitySummary: Strong at reasoning, document analysis, code generation, and following complex instructions.
userFacing.limitations: Data is processed on Anthropic's servers. Not suitable for tasks requiring data to stay on-premise.
userFacing.regulatoryNotes: SOC 2 Type II certified. Suitable for most business data. Consult compliance team for healthcare/financial PII.

name: Claude / Anthropic (OAuth Subscription)
costPerformanceNotes: Uses your Claude Max subscription. Sign in via OAuth or paste a token from 'claude setup-token'. No prompt caching, no 1M context.

userFacing.plainDescription: Anthropic's Claude models accessed through a subscription plan.
userFacing.authExplained: Sign in with your Claude account via OAuth, or paste a subscription token. No separate API key needed.
userFacing.costExplained: Included in your Claude subscription. Limited to Haiku-class models.
userFacing.capabilitySummary: Good at conversation and basic analysis. Limited to smaller models.
userFacing.limitations: Only Haiku-class models available. Cannot access Sonnet or Opus through subscription.
userFacing.regulatoryNotes: Same as Anthropic. SOC 2 Type II certified.

userFacing.plainDescription: Creator of GPT models. Versatile AI for conversation, analysis, and content generation.
userFacing.authExplained: API key — a password you get from their website (platform.openai.com).
userFacing.costExplained: Charges per use. Typical conversations cost less than $0.01. Image generation and advanced models cost more.
userFacing.capabilitySummary: Strong at general conversation, content creation, code, and multimodal tasks (images, voice).
userFacing.limitations: Data is processed on OpenAI's servers. Rate limits can apply during high usage.
userFacing.regulatoryNotes: SOC 2 Type II certified. Enterprise tier available for stricter data handling.

userFacing.plainDescription: Microsoft's enterprise-grade hosting of OpenAI models, running inside your own Azure environment.
userFacing.authExplained: API key or corporate Azure identity. Your IT team provisions access through your Azure subscription.
userFacing.costExplained: Similar per-token rates to OpenAI, billed through your existing Azure account. No separate vendor relationship needed.
userFacing.capabilitySummary: Same GPT models as OpenAI but with enterprise controls: data stays in your Azure tenancy, custom fine-tuning, and SLA guarantees.
userFacing.limitations: Requires an active Azure subscription and deployment setup. More configuration steps than direct OpenAI access.
userFacing.dataResidency: Your chosen Azure region. Data does not leave your Azure tenancy.
userFacing.regulatoryNotes: Inherits Azure compliance certifications (ISO 27001, SOC 2, HIPAA BAA available). Strong choice for regulated industries.

userFacing.plainDescription: Google's AI models, particularly strong at understanding images, documents, and large volumes of content.
userFacing.authExplained: API key — a password you get from Google AI Studio (aistudio.google.com).
userFacing.costExplained: Competitive per-token pricing. A free tier is available for low-volume use. Paid tiers start from fractions of a cent per request.
userFacing.capabilitySummary: Excellent at multimodal tasks (images, PDFs, video frames), long-context documents, and tasks requiring broad general knowledge.
userFacing.limitations: Data is processed on Google's servers. Some models have slower response times for very long documents.
userFacing.dataResidency: United States (Google servers, with some regional options).
userFacing.regulatoryNotes: SOC 2 Type II certified. Google Workspace customers have additional data processing agreements. Verify terms for sensitive data.

userFacing.plainDescription: Amazon's managed AI service giving access to multiple top models — including Claude — running inside your own AWS account.
userFacing.authExplained: AWS IAM credentials managed by your IT team. No separate vendor accounts required for the AI models themselves.
userFacing.costExplained: Billed through your existing AWS account at per-token rates comparable to direct providers. Consolidated AWS billing applies.
userFacing.capabilitySummary: Access to Claude, Llama, and other leading models with AWS-level security controls, VPC isolation, and audit logging.
userFacing.limitations: Requires an AWS account, IAM setup, and Bedrock model access requests. The most complex provider to configure.
userFacing.dataResidency: Your chosen AWS region. Data stays within your AWS account boundary.
userFacing.regulatoryNotes: Inherits AWS compliance certifications (ISO 27001, SOC 2, HIPAA, FedRAMP). Best choice for organisations with strict data sovereignty requirements.

userFacing.plainDescription: AI running entirely on your own hardware via Docker Model Runner. No data leaves your system.
userFacing.authExplained: None needed — it's built into Docker Desktop.
userFacing.costExplained: Costs only the electricity to run your computer. No per-use charges.
userFacing.capabilitySummary: Handles conversation, simple summaries, guided tasks, and basic analysis. Limited by your hardware.
userFacing.limitations: Cannot match cloud models for complex reasoning, large document processing, or specialized tasks.
userFacing.regulatoryNotes: Maximum privacy. Suitable for all sensitivity levels including restricted/regulated data.

userFacing.plainDescription: Elon Musk's AI company. Their Grok models are notable for access to real-time information and current events.
userFacing.authExplained: API key — a password you get from their console (console.x.ai).
userFacing.costExplained: Competitive per-token pricing, comparable to other major providers.
userFacing.capabilitySummary: Strong at tasks requiring up-to-date information, news analysis, and general conversation. Good reasoning in Grok 3.
userFacing.limitations: Smaller ecosystem than OpenAI or Anthropic. Data processed on xAI servers.
userFacing.regulatoryNotes: Relatively newer provider. Review their data processing terms before using for sensitive business data.

userFacing.plainDescription: French AI company known for efficient, multilingual models and a strong commitment to European data standards.
userFacing.authExplained: API key — a password you get from their console (console.mistral.ai).
userFacing.costExplained: Cost-effective pricing, often cheaper than US counterparts for equivalent quality. Charges per token used.
userFacing.capabilitySummary: Excellent at multilingual tasks (especially European languages), code generation, and instruction following. Efficient models that balance cost and quality.
userFacing.limitations: Smaller model range than OpenAI or Anthropic. Less ecosystem tooling available.
userFacing.regulatoryNotes: GDPR-compliant. EU data residency makes this a strong choice for European businesses or those with EU data obligations.

userFacing.plainDescription: AI company specialising in enterprise search, retrieval, and document understanding — built for business workflows.
userFacing.authExplained: API key — a password you get from their dashboard (dashboard.cohere.com).
userFacing.costExplained: Competitive pricing with a free tier for development. Production usage billed per token.
userFacing.capabilitySummary: Exceptional at searching and retrieving information from your own documents and databases. Embedding models enable semantic search across large knowledge bases.
userFacing.limitations: Less suited to open-ended creative tasks compared to GPT or Claude. Focused on retrieval and business use cases.
userFacing.dataResidency: United States (Cohere servers, with cloud provider options).
userFacing.regulatoryNotes: SOC 2 certified. Enterprise agreements available. Well-suited for business document search and knowledge management.

userFacing.plainDescription: Chinese AI company offering highly capable reasoning models at very low cost.
userFacing.authExplained: API key — a password you get from their platform (platform.deepseek.com).
userFacing.costExplained: Among the lowest per-token prices available. Complex reasoning tasks that cost $0.10 elsewhere may cost $0.01 here.
userFacing.capabilitySummary: Strong at mathematics, logic, coding, and structured reasoning tasks. The R1 model rivals top-tier models at a fraction of the cost.
userFacing.limitations: Data is processed on servers in China. Not suitable for any data subject to data sovereignty, export controls, or regulatory restrictions.
userFacing.regulatoryNotes: Data residency in China creates significant compliance concerns for most regulated industries. Not recommended for business, personal, or sensitive data without legal review.

userFacing.plainDescription: Ultra-fast AI inference platform using custom hardware. Responses arrive noticeably faster than standard cloud providers.
userFacing.authExplained: API key — a password you get from their console (console.groq.com).
userFacing.costExplained: Competitive pricing with a free tier. Speed advantage comes at similar or lower cost than OpenAI for equivalent open-source models.
userFacing.capabilitySummary: Best for real-time applications where response speed matters: customer-facing chat, live transcription, and interactive tools. Runs Llama and Mistral models.
userFacing.limitations: Runs open-source models, not proprietary ones like GPT-4 or Claude. Model selection is narrower.
userFacing.regulatoryNotes: SOC 2 Type II certified. Good choice for latency-sensitive applications where open-source models are sufficient.

userFacing.plainDescription: Cloud platform giving access to a large catalogue of open-source AI models in one place.
userFacing.authExplained: API key — a password you get from their platform (api.together.ai).
userFacing.costExplained: Competitive per-token pricing across a wide model selection. Useful for comparing models or accessing specialised ones.
userFacing.capabilitySummary: Access to over 100 open-source models including Llama, Mistral, Qwen, and others. Good for experimentation and finding the right model for a specific task.
userFacing.limitations: All models are open-source; no access to proprietary models like GPT-4 or Claude. Data processed on Together's servers.
userFacing.regulatoryNotes: SOC 2 certified. Suitable for most business use cases. Verify specific model terms for regulated data.

userFacing.plainDescription: Fast and cost-efficient inference platform for open-source models, optimised for production workloads.
userFacing.authExplained: API key — a password you get from their platform (fireworks.ai).
userFacing.costExplained: Among the most cost-effective options for open-source models. Batch processing discounts available for high-volume use.
userFacing.capabilitySummary: Efficient serving of Llama, Mixtral, Qwen, and other open-source models. Good for high-volume, cost-sensitive applications.
userFacing.limitations: Open-source models only. Narrower model selection than Together AI. Data processed on Fireworks servers.
userFacing.regulatoryNotes: SOC 2 certified. Suitable for production business workloads where open-source models meet the capability requirements.

name: OpenAI Codex
costPerformanceNotes: Agentic coding specialist. ~3x cheaper than GPT-4o for code tasks. Runs in sandboxed environment with tool use and persistent threads.

name: ChatGPT (OpenAI Subscription)
costPerformanceNotes: GPT chat models accessed via ChatGPT subscription OAuth. Activated automatically when Codex (OpenAI) is connected.

userFacing.plainDescription: ChatGPT conversation models (GPT-4o) accessed through your OpenAI subscription. Activated automatically when you sign in to Codex.
userFacing.authExplained: Uses the same sign-in as Codex — no separate configuration needed.
userFacing.costExplained: Included with your ChatGPT subscription. No additional per-use charges for standard usage.
userFacing.capabilitySummary: Strong general-purpose conversation, code assistance, document analysis, and creative tasks.
userFacing.limitations: Data processed on OpenAI servers. Rate limits depend on subscription tier.
userFacing.regulatoryNotes: SOC 2 Type II certified. Suitable for most business data.

userFacing.plainDescription: A single API key that gives access to models from many providers — OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and dozens more.
userFacing.authExplained: One API key from OpenRouter (openrouter.ai) replaces the need for separate keys from each provider.
userFacing.costExplained: Charges vary by underlying model used. Pricing is transparent and shown per model. A small routing fee may apply.
userFacing.capabilitySummary: Simplifies AI setup by consolidating many providers behind one key. Useful for testing models or switching between them without reconfiguring credentials.
userFacing.limitations: Adds a routing layer between your data and the underlying provider. Not suitable where direct provider contracts are required.
userFacing.dataResidency: Depends on the underlying provider selected. OpenRouter routes requests but does not store content.
userFacing.regulatoryNotes: Review the terms of the specific underlying provider used. OpenRouter itself is a pass-through; compliance obligations rest with the provider.

catalogEntry.description: Extract structured data from PDFs, spreadsheets, and documents
AI-powered code review and security scanning
Enrich business data with external sources
